name: Guadalaviar - Griegos - circular running by the Fuente Coveta
name_original: Guadalaviar - Griegos – running circular por la Fuente Coveta
region_intro: Jogging around Guadalaviar offers a diverse landscape for runners. The region is characterized by its varied terrain, including river valleys, forested areas, and significant elevation changes, with some routes reaching over 1,800 meters. Trails often pass through picturesque villages and alongside natural water features like waterfalls and springs. This area provides a mix of moderate and challenging running experiences. Best jogging routes around Guadalaviar The most popular jogging route is Fuente del Buey Local Trail, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a steady ascent through natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Cascada del Molino de La Chorrera – running from Tragacete, a moderate 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This route offers views of the Cascada del Molino de La Chorrera waterfall. Local runners also love the Guadalaviar - Griegos - circular running by the Fuente Coveta, an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through rural villages and past natural springs, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Jogging around Guadalaviar is defined by river valleys, pine forests, and notable hills, offering a range of experiences from moderate runs to more challenging ascents. The network provides options for different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. Nearly 20 runners have used komoot to explore Guadalaviar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Guadalaviar?
A: There are currently 5 curated running routes around Guadalaviar on komoot, offering a range of experiences from moderate to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails in Guadalaviar for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31445891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Felipe (1,850 m) – Estrecho del Infierno loop from Tragacete&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16 km route with significant elevation gain, reaching up to 1,850 meters.

Q: Can I find running routes with waterfalls in Guadalaviar?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its natural water features. You can enjoy views of the Cascada del Molino de La Chorrera waterfall on the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014664247/cascada-del-molino-de-la-chorrera-running-from-tragacete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Molino de La Chorrera – running from Tragacete&lt;/a&gt; route. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuervo River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are notable natural monuments in the area.

Q: Are there any circular running paths in Guadalaviar?
A: Yes, several routes offer a loop experience. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983274448/guadalaviar-griegos-circular-running-by-the-fuente-coveta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalaviar - Griegos - circular running by the Fuente Coveta&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.4 km loop that takes you through rural villages and past natural springs. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31446623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Frías de Albarracín&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails around Guadalaviar?
A: Runners in Guadalaviar can expect a diverse landscape featuring river valleys, dense pine forests, and notable hills. Many routes pass through picturesque villages and alongside natural springs, offering a mix of natural beauty and cultural glimpses.

Q: Are there any points of interest or landmarks to see while running in Guadalaviar?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural and historical landmarks. Along your runs, you might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4542067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moricacho of the Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4542170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de las Pisadas&lt;/a&gt;. The charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griegos&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable spot.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Guadalaviar?
A: Guadalaviar offers varied terrain suitable for running across different seasons. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference for weather, the region's diverse landscape with forests and river valleys can provide pleasant conditions in spring and autumn. Summer runs might be best enjoyed in the cooler parts of the day, and winter can offer unique, crisp experiences, especially on lower elevation trails.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass through villages or offer a cultural experience?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983274448/guadalaviar-griegos-circular-running-by-the-fuente-coveta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalaviar - Griegos - circular running by the Fuente Coveta&lt;/a&gt; route is an excellent choice. It leads through rural villages like Griegos and past natural springs, providing a blend of natural beauty and local charm.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Guadalaviar?
A: The komoot community has actively explored Guadalaviar, with nearly 20 runners using the platform to discover its varied terrain. Users often highlight the region's diverse landscapes, from river valleys to pine forests, and the mix of moderate to challenging ascents available.

Q: Are there any moderate-difficulty running routes for a steady workout?
A: Yes, most of the routes in Guadalaviar are classified as moderate, providing a good balance for a steady workout. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983315048/fuente-del-buey-local-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Buey Local Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.1-mile (13 km) route, is a popular moderate option featuring a steady ascent through natural landscapes.

Q: What are the typical distances of running routes in Guadalaviar?
A: The running routes in Guadalaviar vary in length, catering to different preferences. You can find shorter routes like the 4.9 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014664247/cascada-del-molino-de-la-chorrera-running-from-tragacete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Molino de La Chorrera – running from Tragacete&lt;/a&gt;, as well as longer options such as the 13.4 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983274448/guadalaviar-griegos-circular-running-by-the-fuente-coveta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalaviar - Griegos - circular running by the Fuente Coveta&lt;/a&gt;, and the challenging 16 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31445891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Felipe (1,850 m) – Estrecho del Infierno loop from Tragacete&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: La Fuente Coveta is a picnic area and recreational area located in a wonderful environment with amazing tranquility. Located between the towns of Griegos and Guadalaviar, it is a picturesque landscape where tranquility reigns, ideal for unwinding with a walk here.

Griegos is a charming village located in the Albarracín mountain range. 
Griegos is also known for being one of the highest villages in Spain, at 1,601 meters above sea level. It's also a perfect place to enjoy outdoor activities such as horseback riding, hiking, and mountain biking.
